question.text: As per SEBI regulations relating to Registered Investment Advisers (RIA), which of the following statements is correct?

answer.text: Mutual fund distributors can also act as investment advisers
Only qualified professionals licensed by SEBI can act as RIAs
RIAs receive commission from product manufacturers
RIA regulation came into effect in 2020

explanation.text: **The Logic:** As per the SEBI RIA Regulation of 2013, only qualified professionals licensed by SEBI can act as investment advisers. After this regulation, distributors of financial products can no longer claim the title of investment adviser. RIAs are fee-based professionals accountable to their investors. **The Trap:** Before 2013, distributors often acted as advisers, but post-regulation they cannot claim this title. **Cross-Exam Context:** RIA regulations are the core of X-A and X-B exams and also relevant in V-A for understanding the advisory vs distribution distinction. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The key distinction is fiduciary — RIAs must act in the client's best interest, while distributors act as agents of the product manufacturer.
